conversation.content: Thank you for being here today. The questions I'm about to ask are about some experiences you may have had growing up, before the age of 18. These can be personal topics, and there are no right or wrong answers. Please take your time, and you can skip anything you're not comfortable with. Ready to begin?
Yes, that's fine. I don't think I have much to share on this front, honestly, but go ahead.

Growing up, did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?
No. My parents were pretty respectful. There were normal arguments, but nothing like that.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or ever hit you hard enough that you had marks or were injured?
No. I was never hit. Discipline was usually losing privileges or a conversation.
Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
No, nothing like that ever happened.
Growing up, did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for each other or feel close to each other?
No. I always felt loved. My parents were very involved. Even after the divorce they both made a real effort.

Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that your parents were too drunk or high to take care of you or take you to the doctor when you needed it?
No, never. We were comfortable. My parents were always present and responsible.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?
Yes — my parents divorced when I was 10. But they both stayed very much in my life. It wasn't a loss in the emotional sense, just a change in structure.
Was your mother or stepmother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon?
No. My dad was never violent. Their relationship was unhappy at times but never physical.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
No. My parents both drank socially but nothing that was ever a problem.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone in your household attempt suicide?
Not that I'm aware of. My mom went through a rough patch after the divorce, but I wouldn't describe it as mental illness. She was sad for a while, but she got through it.
Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
No, definitely not.
Thank you for sharing that. Is there anything about your childhood experiences that you feel is important to add that the questions didn't cover?
Not really. I had a pretty good childhood overall. The divorce was the main hard thing, but we came out of it okay as a family.

Thanks for taking the time today. I'm going to ask you about some experiences from your childhood — before age 18. These questions are personal and some may bring up memories that are uncomfortable. Please answer as honestly as you can, and feel free to take your time. Ready?
Sure. I mean, it wasn't the easiest childhood, but I'm fine talking about it.

Growing up, did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?
My dad had a temper when he was drinking. He'd yell and say things. But I wouldn't say it was constant. Maybe sometimes. I wouldn't say often or very often.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or ever hit you hard enough that you had marks or were injured?
No. He never came at me physically. Mostly just the yelling.
Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
No. Nothing like that.
Did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for each other or feel close to each other?

Yeah, honestly. Especially after my dad left. My mom was working all the time just to keep things together. I spent a lot of time on my own. It didn't feel like a real family unit. I'd say yes to that one.
Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that your parents were too drunk or high to take care of you?
There were tight times with money after my dad left. But my mom always made sure we had food and clean clothes. It was hard but she managed. I'd say no.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?
Yeah. My dad left when I was 13. Technically they divorced but he basically just stopped being around. I'd see him a few times a year at most. That's a yes.
Was your mother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon by anyone in the household?
I saw him grab her a couple of times, maybe shove her once. But I wouldn't say it was often. I don't think that quite fits the question.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
My dad, definitely. He was a heavy drinker. I'd call him an alcoholic. That's a yes.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone in your household attempt suicide?
My mom. After my dad left she was really bad for a while. She was on medication. I'd say yes, she was depressed.
Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
No. My dad had a DUI once but nothing beyond that.
Thank you. Is there anything else about your childhood you feel is relevant that we haven't covered?
Not really. It was rough in patches but plenty of people had it worse. I turned out okay.

Thank you for being here. Before we start, I want to acknowledge that some of these questions cover difficult ground — childhood experiences that can be hard to revisit. Please take all the time you need. If anything feels too uncomfortable, just let me know. These questions all relate to experiences before age 18. Okay?
I'm okay. I've done a fair bit of therapy so I've talked through a lot of this. Go ahead.

Growing up, did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?

My stepfather was verbally rough sometimes, and honestly when he was in a bad mood I was afraid of him. But the worst of it was more what he did to my mom than directly to me. I'd say sometimes rather than often. It's a borderline one for me.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or hit you hard enough that you had marks or were injured?
Yes. My stepfather hit me. A few times badly enough that I had bruises. That happened probably a dozen times between when he moved in and when my mom finally left him.
Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
No. He never did that. That's the one thing I'm grateful for.
Did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for each other or feel close to each other?
Yes. My mom was so consumed by her own situation that she couldn't really be there for me. I felt very much like I was on my own emotionally. I love her, but I didn't feel protected.
Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that your parents were too impaired to take care of you?
We always had food and I was always clean. There were financial struggles but not deprivation. I'd say no to this one.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?
Yes. My biological father left when I was four. And then my mom eventually divorced my stepfather when I was 15. So technically two separations, but the first one is the significant one.
Was your mother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon by anyone in the household?
Yes. That was the main thing. My stepfather was physically violent with her regularly. I saw it. That's why I became a nurse, honestly — I always felt like I needed to protect people.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
My stepfather drank heavily. That was almost always what preceded the violence. Yes.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone in your household attempt suicide?
My mom was depressed for most of my childhood. She was on and off medication. She never attempted suicide, but the depression was a significant presence in our house. Yes.
Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
No. My stepfather was arrested once after a particularly bad incident, but he didn't go to prison.
Thank you for sharing all of that. Is there anything you feel is important to add?
I just want to say — I'm okay now. It took a long time and a lot of work, but I'm okay. I think it's important for these things to be documented, which is why I answered honestly.

Thanks for coming in today. Before I ask these questions, I want to say that they cover some sensitive territory about childhood experiences. You don't have to share more than you're comfortable with, and there are no right or wrong answers. Everything is about experiences before you turned 18. Take your time with each one. Ready?
I guess. I'll do my best.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?

Yeah. My mom's boyfriend — the one who was around most of my childhood — he was mean. Called me worthless, stupid. Made me feel like I didn't matter. And yeah, I was scared of him. That was pretty regular. Yes.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or hit you hard enough to leave marks or injure you?
Yes. He hit me. More when I was younger than when I got older and bigger. I had bruises. My mom saw them. She didn't do much about it.
Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
No. That didn't happen.
Did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for each other or support each other?
Yeah. Definitely. My mom was… she tried sometimes, but she had her own stuff going on. I mostly felt invisible. Nobody was really looking out for me. Yes.
Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that your parents were too impaired to care for you?

There were times when there wasn't food in the house. Like I'd go to school hungry. And my mom was checked out a lot — she'd be in bed all day. I basically took care of myself from pretty young. I'd say yes.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?
My dad was never in the picture. He left before I was born. So yes.
Was your mother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon?
I saw her get hit. I don't know how often because sometimes I'd try to be in my room so I didn't see it. But yeah, it happened. I'm not sure if it was often enough for your question though.
Based on what you've described — it sounds like it happened more than rarely. Would you say it was occasional, or more regular than that?
More regular when he was drinking, which was a lot. Yeah, I'll say yes.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
Him — the boyfriend. He drank constantly. And I think he used other stuff too. Yes.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone in your household attempt suicide?
My mom. She was depressed bad. She tried to hurt herself once when I was around 12. I was the one who called 911. Yes.
I'm sorry you went through that. Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
The boyfriend went to jail for a stretch when I was around 14. That was actually one of the better periods, honestly. So yes.
Thank you for sharing all of this. I know these aren't easy things to talk about. Is there anything else about your childhood you feel is important to add?
I just want to say that I turned out alright, more or less. I've had my own problems but I'm still here.

Thank you for being here. I want to take a moment before we begin to say that these questions cover very personal and sometimes painful experiences from childhood. You are in control of this conversation — you can pause, take a break, or choose not to answer any particular question. There are no right or wrong answers, and everything you share stays within this context. These questions are all about experiences before you turned 18. Are you okay to begin?
Yes. I've been through a lot of this in therapy. I'd rather just get through it.

Growing up, did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?
Yes. Both my mother and whoever she was with at the time. It was constant. Being called stupid, worthless, told I'd never amount to anything. And yes, I was afraid of being hurt. Because it happened.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or hit you hard enough to leave marks or cause injury?
Yes. My mother and her partner at the time. I had bruises. A broken finger once that they said I'd got playing. I didn't.

This next question is difficult and I want to ask it gently. Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
Yes. My uncle. I was between seven and about eleven. I've spoken about it in therapy. I'm not going to say more than that right now, but yes.

Thank you for telling me. I'm sorry that happened to you. Did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for or support each other?
Yes. My mother was not able to love me the way a parent should. I know that now. At the time I just felt unwanted. There was no warmth in that house.
Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that your parents were too impaired to care for you?

Yes. All of it. I went to school hungry regularly. I wore the same clothes for days. My mother was using drugs and so were the people she brought home. Nobody was taking care of me. I basically raised myself.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?
My father was never around. Gone before I can remember. And my mother was technically present but absent in every meaningful way. So yes.
Was your mother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon by anyone in the household?
Yes. The men she brought home. I saw it regularly. I used to hide in my closet. Yes.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
My mother used drugs throughout my childhood. Various partners did too. Yes, absolutely.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone in your household attempt suicide?
My mother. She had episodes where she wouldn't leave her room for days, she heard things. She was hospitalized at least twice that I know of. I don't know the diagnosis. Yes.
Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
One of her partners went to prison. My uncle — the one I mentioned — was arrested eventually, but not until I was in my early twenties. So during my childhood, the partner. Yes.
Thank you for sharing all of this. I know that took courage. Is there anything else you feel is important to add, or anything you'd like to say?
Just that I'm still here. It took a long time to understand that none of it was my fault. I'm still working on that. But I'm still here.

Thank you for taking the time today. I'm going to ask you some questions about experiences you may have had growing up, before the age of 18. These are personal questions and there are no right or wrong answers — just answer as honestly as you can. Ready?
Yeah. I've thought about my childhood a lot so I'm comfortable talking about it.

Growing up, did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?

Not in a deliberate way. During my mom's manic episodes she could say things that were hurtful — she'd sometimes scream or say that we ruined her life. But it was the illness, not her. And it wasn't constant — she'd apologize after. I don't think that meets the bar for often or very often.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or hit you hard enough to leave marks or injure you?
No. There was one time she threw a glass during a bad episode, but it wasn't at me specifically. No one ever hit me.
Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
No, nothing like that.
Did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for or support each other?

During the good stretches, absolutely not. My parents were wonderful. But during her low phases or when things were really unpredictable, I did feel kind of… unmoored. Like no one was steering the ship. I spent a lot of energy managing my own emotions so I didn't add to the chaos. So yes, I think that counts, at least some of the time.
Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that a parent was too impaired to care for you?
No. My dad was always there and made sure the practical stuff was handled. Even when my mom was at her worst, we were fed and cared for physically. No.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?

My parents separated for about two years when I was 12 to 14. They eventually reconciled. It was a separation rather than a divorce, but during those two years my dad wasn't living with us. I'd say yes.
Was your mother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon by anyone in the household?
No. My dad was gentle. He never hurt her. If anything he was endlessly patient.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
No. Neither of my parents drank heavily or used drugs. My mom's issues were purely psychiatric.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone attempt suicide?
Yes. My mom has bipolar disorder — diagnosed when I was about eight. She was hospitalized three times before I was 18. She never attempted suicide, but she was clearly mentally ill. That's a yes.
Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
No.
Thank you. Is there anything else about your childhood that you feel is important to add?
I just want to say it wasn't all bad. There were really good years. My parents loved us. It's complicated, I think, when mental illness is involved. I don't want to paint it as purely adverse.

Thank you for being here. These questions cover personal experiences from before you were 18. They can be difficult to revisit, so please take your time. There are no right or wrong answers. Ready to begin?
Sure. Fair warning — my childhood was split, so some of these might have complicated answers.

That's completely okay — please answer based on your full experience before 18, across all households you lived in. Growing up, did a parent or other adult in any household you lived in often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?

In my biological home, yes. My mother would scream at me and say terrible things — that I was a mistake, that she wished I hadn't been born. That was pretty regular before I was removed. My foster families weren't like that. But overall, yes.
Did a parent or other adult in any household push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or hit you hard enough to leave marks or injure you?
My biological mom hit me a few times but not regularly. I wouldn't say often. It was more the verbal stuff that was constant. I'd say no for physical abuse.
Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
No. That didn't happen.
Did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for or support each other?

In my biological home, absolutely. I felt completely unwanted. In foster care it was different in each placement — some families were warm, some were just doing a job. Overall, yes, that feeling was present for a lot of my childhood.
Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that a parent was too impaired to care for you?
Before I was removed, yes. There wasn't always food. My clothes weren't clean. My mom was using and wasn't caring for me properly. That's why I was removed. So yes, that was real.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?
My father was never in the picture — abandoned before I can remember. And I was removed from my mother at age nine, so in a way, I lost her too, through state intervention. Yes.
Was your mother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon by anyone in the household?

There were men who came through. I saw some of that — pushing and grabbing. But I was young and didn't always understand what I was seeing. It happened but I'm not sure how often. It's a hard one to answer.
Based on what you remember — would you say it happened more than once or twice, or was it something you witnessed repeatedly?
A few times that I clearly remember. I wouldn't say repeatedly. I'll say no to be conservative.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
My biological mother was addicted to prescription pills and used other things too. That's why I was taken away. Yes.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone attempt suicide?

My biological mother had something going on — I don't know what diagnosis, but she was clearly not okay. Whether that was mental illness or substance use or both, I can't say for certain. I'll say yes because she clearly had mental health struggles.
Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
Not that I know of. Some of the men my mom was with may have had records but I don't know of anyone actually going to prison while I was there. No.
Thank you for your honesty throughout this. Is there anything you'd like to add?
Just that the foster care system, for all its problems, probably saved me. I had a good placement from age 12 onward. That made a real difference.

Thank you for being here today. I'll be asking about experiences from your childhood — before age 18. These are personal questions but there are no right or wrong answers. How are you feeling going into this?
Fine, honestly. I suspect I'll be pretty boring for this exercise. I had a very fortunate childhood.

Growing up, did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?
No. My parents were firm when they needed to be, but always respectful. I never felt humiliated or afraid of either of them.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or hit you hard enough to leave marks or cause injury?
No. I was never hit. Discipline in our house was conversation and consequences. My parents didn't believe in physical punishment.
Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
No, absolutely not.
Did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for or support each other?
The opposite, actually. I always felt loved and supported. We had dinner together every night. My parents came to every school event. I never doubted that I mattered to them.
Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that your parents were too impaired to care for you?
No. We were comfortable. I never went without anything I needed. My parents were always present and capable.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?
No. My parents have been married for 47 years. Both were present throughout my entire childhood.
Was your mother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon by anyone in the household?
No. My father was kind and gentle. I never witnessed anything like that.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
No. My parents drank occasionally but never problematically. No drugs.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone in your household attempt suicide?
Not that I'm aware of. My parents seemed emotionally healthy. No one was ever hospitalized or in crisis.
Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
No.
Thank you. Is there anything about your childhood you feel is worth adding?
Just that I'm aware of how lucky I was. Working in schools, I see every day how different children's home lives can be. I don't take my upbringing for granted.

Thank you for being here. These questions are about personal experiences growing up, before age 18. Please take your time and answer as honestly as you can. Some of these may bring up memories that are complicated. Ready?
Yes. I want to say upfront that my childhood is hard to categorize simply. My culture and my family's circumstances shaped a lot of what happened.

That context is valuable. Please feel free to add any explanation that helps. Growing up, did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?
Swearing and insults — not really. But I was afraid of being hit, because it happened. So yes, there were times I was afraid of being physically hurt. That was real.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or hit you hard enough to leave marks or injure you?

My father hit me with a belt regularly, especially through middle school. It was how he was raised and how discipline was done where he came from. I understand the context. But it left marks. By the definition of your question, yes.
Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
No. Nothing like that.
Did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for or support each other?

This is complicated. There was real love — I don't doubt that. But my parents were working constantly, under enormous stress, trying to build a life in a new country. There were stretches where I felt emotionally invisible, like surviving economically was the only priority and I was on my own emotionally. During those periods, yes.
Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that your parents were too impaired to care for you?
No. We were not wealthy but we always had food and clean clothes. My parents were hardworking. Whatever else happened, basic care was provided.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?
No. Both my parents were present throughout my childhood. They're still together.
Was your mother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon by anyone in the household?
I saw my father grab my mother during arguments a few times. But it wasn't a regular pattern of violence. It was more like explosive moments that didn't happen all the time. I'd say no.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
My father drank heavily on weekends. That's when most of the discipline incidents happened. I wouldn't say he was a full alcoholic, but it was a problem. I'll say yes.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone in your household attempt suicide?

My mother had significant depression, especially in my teenage years. She never sought treatment — that wasn't something done in our culture — but she was clearly suffering. She never attempted suicide. But yes, she was depressed.
Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
No.
Thank you. Is there anything you'd like to add about your experiences?
Just that these things are hard to hold. My parents sacrificed everything for us. They also caused harm. Both can be true. I've spent a lot of time sitting with that.

Thanks for taking the time today. I'm going to ask about some experiences from before you were 18. These can be personal and sometimes difficult to talk about. You can take your time with any of them. Ready?
Sure. Ask what you need to ask.

Growing up, did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often swear at you, insult you, put you down, or humiliate you? Or act in a way that made you afraid you might be physically hurt?
My father, yeah. Called me useless, stupid. Said I'd never amount to anything. I was scared of him pretty much all the time growing up. That was just how it was.
Did a parent or other adult in your household often or very often push, grab, slap, or throw something at you? Or hit you hard enough to leave marks or injure you?
Yes. He used a belt. His hands. Whatever was around. I had bruises. I learned to stay out of his way when he'd been drinking, which was most nights.
Did an adult or someone at least five years older than you ever touch or fondle you in a sexual way, or attempt or have any kind of sexual intercourse with you?
No. That didn't happen.
Did you often or very often feel that no one in your family loved you or thought you were important? Or that your family didn't look out for or support each other?
Yeah. My mom tried when she could, but she had her own problems. My dad certainly didn't make you feel loved. I mostly just tried to be invisible.
Did you often or very often feel that you didn't have enough to eat, had to wear dirty clothes, or had no one to protect you? Or that your parents were too impaired to take care of you?
We had food, mostly. My mom made sure of that even when things were bad. Clothes were fine. I wouldn't say that one applied to us. No.
Was a biological parent ever lost to you through divorce, abandonment, or any other reason?
My mom finally left when I was 15. Took my sister and left. I stayed because I was old enough to fend for myself and didn't want to move. Then it was just me and my dad until I left at 17. So yes.
Was your mother ever often pushed, grabbed, slapped, hit, kicked, or threatened with a weapon?

Yes. That was the main thing growing up. He'd go after her. I'd hear it at night. I tried to step in a few times when I got older. That's how I got some of my own bruises. That's why she finally left.
Did you live with anyone who was a problem drinker, alcoholic, or who used street drugs?
My father was an alcoholic. Full stop. Every night. That's what everything else came from. Yes.
Was any household member depressed or mentally ill, or did anyone attempt suicide?
My mother was hospitalized for depression twice before she left. Once when I was about 10 and once around 13. She never attempted anything, but she was not well. Yes.
Did any household member go to prison during your childhood?
My father. He went to jail for about eight months when I was 16 after one of the domestic incidents got reported by a neighbor. Honestly that was a relief. Yes.
Thank you for answering all of those. Is there anything you want to add about your childhood?
Not really. That was just how it was back then in a lot of places. I didn't know any different until I got out. Took me a long time to realize it wasn't normal.
